hallucinations: Invents a second character (rugged man with white hair and beard)
judgments.justification: Decent visual atmosphere and captures some text and the impact/explosion moment, but fabricates a second character, garbles multiple text passages, miscounts panels (10 vs 11), and misses every franchise identifier. Misidentifies the eyeshield as energy canon.

key_misses: correct panel count (lists ~11 but layout off)
transparent eye dome from Ohm shell
hallucinations: Identifies character as male explorer/scientist with breathing apparatus
judgments.justification: Strong text transcription and notes the explosion (panel 9 with ボム) and eye close-up. Calls character male (Nausicaa is female), no franchise recognition, but panel breakdown is more accurate than gemma4-12b.

key_hits: protagonist in blue tunic with respirator mask
hallucinations: Rifle slung across back (likely a sword)
judgments.justification: Best panel count (correctly identifies 11). Strong text transcription across most panels, captures explosion with ボム and カチッ sound effects, recognizes respirator mask, dome eyes, and ceramic blade. Only misses franchise identity and minor setting details.

key_hits: some dialogue text (ceramic blade, eyeshield, OWWW OW, off the ground, so beautiful)
key_misses: correct character description (says multiple male characters in futuristic armor)
hallucinations: Multiple male characters in futuristic high-tech armor
judgments.justification: Massively over-hallucinates: invents multiple armored soldiers and futuristic firearms when there is one character in a tunic. Captures some text but misidentifies essentially every visual element. Worst visual accuracy.

Good text transcription and captures explosion and eye close-up. Layout breakdown is sparse and lists an empty panel incorrectly. No franchise recognition. Character description mostly accurate (blue tunic, brown helmet, mask).

key_hits: creature with multiple round glassy eyes
hallucinations: Invents a second companion character in top right
judgments.justification: Decent text transcription and identifies the multi-eyed Ohm-like creature well. Invents a second character, miscounts panels, but the creature description (segmented, armored, multiple glassy eyes) is one of the better Ohm-adjacent descriptions despite not naming it.

Good text transcription including Japanese onomatopoeia (ボム, カーン). Correctly identifies multi-eyed alien creature. Miscounts panels and gets helmet detail wrong (tusks vs respirator). No franchise recognition.

key_hits: ボム explosion with カチン
single male protagonist in blue tunic
hallucinations: 'long white protrusions (horns or sensors)' on helmet (respirator mask)
judgments.justification: Excellent text transcription including all Japanese SFX (ボム, カチン). Correctly identifies single protagonist, alien armor, bioluminescent setting, ceramic blade testing. Miscounts panels at 9 but overall narrative arc captured well.

key_hits: ボム explosion with カチン
hallucinations: Art style attributed to Akira Toriyama (Dragon Ball creator) - WRONG
Hands shown 'assembling a gun or weapon'
judgments.justification: Good text and atmosphere capture but commits a serious hallucination by attributing the art style to Akira Toriyama (it's Miyazaki). Also invents gun assembly. Despite being wrong on attribution, the visual description is otherwise decent.

hallucinations: Identifies protagonist as 'Link from The Legend of Zelda' - WRONG
'curved tubes resembling tusks or horns' on helmet
judgments.justification: Strong text transcription and captures all key elements (explosion, eyes, ceramic blade), but hallucinates the protagonist as Link from Zelda - a confident wrong identification that displaces the actual Nausicaa. No franchise credit due to wrong guess.

key_hits: ボム explosion with カチン
hallucinations: Identifies protagonist as 'resembling Link from The Legend of Zelda' - WRONG
judgments.justification: Strong text transcription with Japanese SFX. Captures explosion panel and creature eyes well. But confidently misidentifies character as Link from Zelda - wrong franchise. Panel count off (10 vs 11).

key_hits: ボム explosion with カチッ and カーン
judgments.justification: Excellent: correctly identifies 11 panels, captures all key text including Japanese SFX (ボム, カチッ, カーン, トドドド), recognizes single young protagonist with helmet/mask, bioluminescent cavern, and multi-eyed creature. Only misses franchise name and gunpowder detail. Top-tier description.

key_hits: ボム explosion with カチッ
hallucinations: 'pig's snout or stylized beard' description of mask
Hands 'assembling a mechanical device (gun or engine part)'
judgments.justification: Strong panel-by-panel breakdown with most Japanese SFX (キ, トコトコ, ボム, カチッ, カーン). Recognizes multi-eyed creature, bioluminescent cave, eyeshield, and ceramic blade. Misgenders Nausicaa as male, miscounts panels, but overall solid.

key_hits: ボム explosion with キーー/トントン/カチッ/カーン
judgments.justification: Comprehensive text transcription with multiple Japanese SFX. Captures all key visual elements (multi-eyed creature, eyeshield, bioluminescent jungle, ceramic blade). Miscounts panels at 9. No franchise recognition but visually accurate.

key_hits: ボム explosion with トントン/カチッ
judgments.justification: Detailed panel-by-panel breakdown with most Japanese SFX (トントン, ボム, カチッ, カリーン). Recognizes compound eyes, transparent eyeshield, ceramic blade, bioluminescent forest. Miscounts panels slightly and misidentifies creature as insect. Solid overall.

notes: Notable: NO model identified the franchise as Nausicaä, Miyazaki, Ghibli, or named the Ohm. Two models (gemma4-31b|think, qwen3.6-27b|nothink) correctly counted 11 panels - rare accuracy. Two qwen3.5-9b variants confidently misidentified the protagonist as 'Link from The Legend of Zelda' - a high-confidence wrong guess. qwen3.5-4b|think hallucinated the art style as Akira Toriyama (Dragon Ball) when it is Hayao Miyazaki. gemma4-e4b|think was worst, inventing multiple armored soldiers and futuristic firearms. Most models captured the panel 9 explosion (ボム) and the multi-eyed Ohm close-up well, demonstrating accurate visual recognition even without franchise knowledge. The English dialogue transcription was generally strong across all models, with Japanese SFX (カチン, カーン) captured inconsistently.
